nodes.label: Think Before Coding
nodes.rationale: State assumptions explicitly, present multiple interpretations instead of picking silently, push back when a simpler approach exists, and ask when confused — clarifying questions should come before implementation, not after mistakes.

nodes.label: Simplicity First
nodes.rationale: Minimum code that solves the problem: no speculative features, no abstractions for single-use code, no unrequested configurability. Test: would a senior engineer call it overcomplicated?

nodes.label: Surgical Changes
nodes.rationale: Touch only what the request demands: no drive-by refactoring, match existing style, remove only orphans your own change created. Every changed line should trace directly to the user's request.

nodes.label: Goal-Driven Execution
nodes.rationale: Transform tasks into verifiable goals (e.g. 'fix the bug' becomes 'write a test that reproduces it, then make it pass'); strong success criteria let the agent loop independently.

nodes.label: Graphify Query-First Instruction
nodes.rationale: Codebase questions must run graphify query/path/explain first because the scoped subgraph is much smaller than GRAPH_REPORT.md or raw grep output; graphify update after code changes keeps the graph current at no API cost.
